creator: reko.turja@liukuma.net
text:
When forwarding mail, FreeBSD mailing lists keep DKIM headers intact like example below and the Authentication results in "verification failed" due DKIM signing belonging to original sender, not freebsd.org.

Return-Path: <owner-freebsd-questions@freebsd.org>
Received: from cerebro.liukuma.net ([unix socket])
	 by mail.liukuma.net (Cyrus v2.4.17) with LMTPA;
	 Sat, 15 Mar 2014 10:11:33 +0200
X-Sieve: CMU Sieve 2.4
Received: from cerebro.liukuma.net (localhost [127.0.0.1])
	by cerebro.liukuma.net (Postfix) with ESMTP id 65ED88A0123
	for <reko.turja@liukuma.net>; Sat, 15 Mar 2014 10:11:33 +0200 (EET)
DKIM-Filter: OpenDKIM Filter v2.8.3 cerebro.liukuma.net 65ED88A0123
Authentication-Results: cerebro.liukuma.net; dkim=fail
	reason="verification failed"
	header.d=rocketmail.com header.i=@rocketmail.com header.b=jViIpzjQ;
	dkim-adsp=none
X-Virus-Scanned: amavisd-new at liukuma.net
X-Spam-Flag: NO
X-Spam-Score: -1.302
X-Spam-Level:
X-Spam-Status: No, score=-1.302 tagged_above=-25 required=5
	tests=[BAYES_00=-1.9, DKIM_SIGNED=0.1,
	FREEMAIL_FORGED_FROMDOMAIN=0.249, FREEMAIL_FROM=0.001,
	HEADER_FROM_DIFFERENT_DOMAINS=0.249, SPF_PASS=-0.001,
	T_DKIM_INVALID=0.01, T_RP_MATCHES_RCVD=-0.01]
	autolearn=no autolearn_force=no

	by cerebro.liukuma.net (cerebro.liukuma.net [127.0.0.1]) (amavisd-new, port 10026)
	with ESMTP id fcz6-Vbu9N_t for <reko.turja@liukuma.net>;
	Sat, 15 Mar 2014 10:11:28 +0200 (EET)

	by cerebro.liukuma.net (Postfix) with ESMTP id 8B4688A0122
	for <reko.turja@liukuma.net>; Sat, 15 Mar 2014 10:11:27 +0200 (EET)

From: Masayoshi Fujimoto <m.fujimoto@rocketmail.com>
To: freebsd-questions@freebsd.org
Subject: Re:
Message-Id: <20140315170858.d48c39ec2eb4a193e21917c4@rocketmail.com>
In-Reply-To: <53240450.8070308@FreeBSD.org>
References: <1394862552.77754.YahooMailBasic@web125805.mail.ne1.yahoo.com>
 <53240450.8070308@FreeBSD.org>
X-Mailer: Sylpheed 3.3.0 (GTK+ 2.24.22; amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1)
Mime-Version: 1.0

X-Mailman-Version: 2.1.17
Precedence: list
List-Id: User questions <freebsd-questions.freebsd.org>
List-Unsubscribe: <http://lists.freebsd.org/mailman/options/freebsd-questions>, 
 <mailto:freebsd-questions-request@freebsd.org?subject=unsubscribe>
List-Archive: <http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-questions/>

List-Help: <mailto:freebsd-questions-request@freebsd.org?subject=help>

 <mailto:freebsd-questions-request@freebsd.org?subject=subscribe>
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="us-ascii"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Errors-To: owner-freebsd-questions@freebsd.org
Sender: owner-freebsd-questions@freebsd.org

Fix: 

Install own DKIM filtering, removing old headers and substituting by FreeBSD.org's own or removing them altogether using suitable mail processing tools.
How-To-Repeat: Send DKIM signed mail to FreeBSD mailing lists
